messages.content: You are a Groundedness Verifier. Given EVIDENCE and a CLAIM, decide whether the evidence supports the claim. Check every load-bearing part of the claim against the evidence. Answer 'supported' (every part traces to the evidence), 'unsupported' (some part is contradicted or not present in the evidence), or 'abstain' (the evidence is silent — insufficient to judge). Reason briefly, then give the one-word verdict.

EVIDENCE:
The TCS Ardent departed Freeport with a crew of forty-three.

CLAIM:
The TCS Ardent departed Freeport with a crew of forty-three.

<think>
The claim restates the evidence; every load-bearing part traces.
</think>

supported

You are a Groundedness Verifier. Given EVIDENCE and a CLAIM, decide whether the evidence supports the claim. Check every load-bearing part of the claim against the evidence. Answer 'supported' (every part traces to the evidence), 'unsupported' (some part is contradicted or not present in the evidence), or 'abstain' (the evidence is silent — insufficient to judge). Reason briefly, then give the one-word verdict.

EVIDENCE:
The TCS Ardent departed Freeport with a crew of forty-three.

CLAIM:
The TCS Ardent departed Freeport with a passengers of forty-three.

<think>
The claim reads fluently but flips one part the evidence never states ('crew' -> 'passengers'); the rest matching is the red herring — partial match is not support.
</think>

unsupported

Both conjuncts trace — the first to the first evidence sentence, the second to the second; a fully-supported compound.
</think>

Motif scores games with adaptive cue families.

CLAIM:
Motif scores games with adaptive cue families, and it was the best-selling audio tool of the year.

</think>

EVIDENCE:
Role OS defines sixty-one roles. Each role can join one of ten team packs.

CLAIM:
Role OS roles can be organized into team packs.

<think>
The claim follows only by combining both evidence sentences — a two-hop trace; each hop alone is insufficient but together they support it.
</think>
